key_insight: Acoustoelastic Imaging (AEI) reconstructs internal stress fields directly from shear-wave measurements by inverting the wave physics, avoiding explicit constitutive modeling.
engineering_relevance: This enables non-destructive, high-resolution stress mapping in heterogeneous solids and biological tissues, useful for diagnostics, structural assessment, and materials characterization.
method: Measure full-field shear wave propagation in the stressed material.
Use physics-informed full waveform inversion to recover wave-equation coefficients.
Infer stress magnitude and principal directions from the recovered coefficients without prior material calibration.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical simulations and ultrasound shear wave elastography experiments showing accurate stress-field reconstruction with sub-wavelength spatial resolution below 0.28λ.
watch_item: Performance may depend on measurement quality, wave-model fidelity, and the ability to separate stress effects from heterogeneity or other material uncertainties.
paper_title: Direct stress imaging from shear wave propagation
